html:
[4446]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 本荥阳人寓于寿春郡 - Originally from Xingyang but residing in Shouchun Prefecture.
  • 假摄丞尉 - Acting as a deputy county magistrate and county magistrate.
  • 求食婚李氏 - Married into the Li family for sustenance.
  • 犹子也 - A younger brother or son-in-law.
  • 心痛疾 - Heartache or chest pain; could be physical or metaphorical.
  • 如狂言拜于空云 - Spoke wildly and bowed to the air; suggests delirium or hallucination.
  • 唯心尚暖耳 - Only the heart was still warm; implies critical condition.
  • 五更鸡鸣一声 - At the first crow of the rooster in the fifth watch; specific time in the early morning.
  • 怕惧行亦不觉甚难 - Feeling fear and anxiety, yet not finding the journey too difficult.
  • 一城郭 - A city or walled town.
  • 似曹官之軰 - Resembling a Cao official; possibly from the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 读元追之由 - Read the original reason for the summons.
  • 居欲得马氏托生 - Desires to be reborn as the wife of Liu.
  • 平生所有 - All that I have in my lifetime; possessions or experiences accumulated over one's life.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 郑洁 - The name 'Zheng Jie' is a proper noun and should be retained as such.
  • 荥阳 - A historical county in Henan Province, China.
  • 寿春郡 - A historical prefecture in Anhui Province, China.
  • 开成五年 - The fifth year of the Kai Cheng era (840 CE) during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 曹官 - Refers to officials from the Cao family, possibly from the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 托生 - The concept of rebirth or reincarnation in Chinese Buddhism and Taoism.
  • 剔其腹 - A violent act, possibly referring to dismemberment or mutilation.
  • 五藏 - Refers to the five internal organs (heart, liver, spleen, lungs, and kidneys) in traditional Chinese medicine.

Proper Nouns

  • 郑洁 - The name of the main subject.
  • 李氏 - The name of the family Zheng Jie married into.
  • 马氏 - The name of the woman whose spirit is involved in the story.
  • 曹官 - A reference to officials from the Cao family.

[4447]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 与作功德为计即可也 - A phrase meaning 'it is sufficient to consider making merit and virtue as a plan.'
  • 若今追某徒寘于无间狱 - A phrase meaning 'if one were to pursue a certain disciple and place them in the hell of no respite.'
  • 亦何裨于马氏哉 - A rhetorical question meaning 'what benefit would it bring to the Ma family?'
  • 本司云此则自辨之须臾 - A phrase meaning 'the official said that this must be self-explained in a moment.'
  • 马氏者到李恐马氏无礼遂对官人云何得如此狡毒 - A complex sentence meaning 'the Ma family, when they reached Li, feared that the Ma family would be disrespectful, so they said to the official, 'How could you be so treacherously cruel?'
  • 李具以私中之言对之 - A phrase meaning 'Li fully responded with private words.'
  • 何如 - A phrase meaning 'what do you think?'
  • 寃系多年别罪受毕令归生路无计伏取裁断 - A complex sentence meaning 'having been wrongfully imprisoned for many years, having completed other crimes, and having no way to return to life, they submit to your judgment.'
  • 且请检某算寿几何 - A phrase meaning 'let us first check the calculation of his lifespan.'
  • 若未合来即请依前说 - A conditional phrase meaning 'if it is not yet time for him to come, then please follow the previous statement.'
  • 若合命尽伏听处分 - A conditional phrase meaning 'if his fate is complete, then we will listen to your instructions.'
  • 灼然有理 - A phrase meaning 'it is clearly reasonable.'
  • 遂召司命须臾一主者抱案入来云 - A phrase meaning 'so he summoned the life recorder, and after a moment, a supervisor came in with a case and said.'
  • 李未合来昨追时已检讫须臾更检检出捧呈官云 - A complex sentence meaning 'Li was not supposed to come; when he was summoned yesterday, it was already checked. After a moment, they checked again and presented the result to the official.'
  • 更有十八年合在人间本司云且令随衙勘责夜则放归耳 - A phrase meaning 'there are still eighteen years left for him to be in the human world. The official said, 'Let him follow the office for inspection and punishment, and at night, he will be released.'
  • 彼处欲夜所司放出似梦而归也 - A phrase meaning 'when it is night, the authorities will release him, as if he is returning from a dream.'
  • 自是人间日暮追使即来鸡鸣即放回如常矣 - A phrase meaning 'from then on, when it is evening in the human world, the summons will come, and when it is cockcrow, he will be released, as usual.'
  • 郑虽贫苦百计只待来使三五日后使人慙谢 - A complex sentence meaning 'Zheng, though poor and suffering, did everything in his power to wait for the messenger. After three or five days, the messenger came and felt ashamed to apologize.'
  • 百味之物深所反侧然不 - A phrase meaning 'all kinds of delicious things deeply troubled him, but he did not.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 功德 - Refers to merit and virtue, important concepts in Buddhism.
  • 无间狱 - Refers to the hell of no respite in Buddhist cosmology, a place of severe punishment.
  • 马氏 - Refers to the Ma family, a specific family in the context.
  • 本司 - Refers to the official or authority in charge.
  • 司命 - Refers to the life recorder, a figure in Chinese mythology who records the lifespan of individuals.
  • 人间 - Refers to the human world, as opposed to the afterlife.
  • 郑 - Refers to Zheng, a specific individual in the context.
  • 百味之物 - Refers to all kinds of delicious things, suggesting a luxurious lifestyle.

Proper Nouns

  • 马氏 - The Ma family.
  • 李 - Li, a specific individual.
  • 郑 - Zheng, a specific individual.

[4448]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 如赐茶浆水粥耳茶酒不如赐浆水 - Elliptical phrase comparing various items, with water being the preferred choice.
  • 贫居之易办 - Phrase meaning 'easy to manage in poverty,' indicating that certain tasks are simpler when resources are limited.
  • 毎晚则备浆水及粥纸钱三五张 - Phrase describing a routine action: 'every evening, prepare water and porridge along with three or five pieces of paper money.'
  • 月十日后毎来皆语言商议出拔李氏 - Phrase indicating a regular meeting to plan assistance: 'after ten days each month, they would come and discuss how to help Li Shi.'
  • 李氏初毎归来并不敢言 - Phrase meaning 'Li Shi, at first, would not dare to speak upon returning,' indicating a period of silence or caution.
  • 自使人同和兼许微说间事 - Phrase describing a controlled environment: 'he would send someone to harmonize and allow a slight mention of private matters.'
  • 常言人罪之重者无如枉法杀人而取金帛 - Phrase emphasizing the gravity of certain crimes: 'he often said that the heaviest sin is to pervert the law to kill and take gold and silk.'
  • 又曰布施者不必造佛寺不如先救骨肉间饥寒 - Phrase highlighting the importance of immediate family support: 'he also said that those who give alms do not necessarily need to build temples; it is better to first help family members in need.'
  • 如有余即分锡类更有余则救街衢间也其福最大 - Phrase emphasizing the moral value of helping others: 'if there is any surplus, distribute it to relatives; if there is even more, help those on the streets; this brings the greatest merit.'
  • 郑君兼凭问还往间一人寿命官爵回报云此人好受金帛今被折寿已欲尽矣然更有一官如能改即得终此秩若踵前则不离任矣 - Complex phrase discussing the consequences of accepting bribes and the possibility of redemption: 'Mr. Zheng, who relied on him to ask about the lifespan and official rank of a person, received a reply that this person likes to accept gold and silk, and his life has been shortened. However, if he changes his ways, he can complete his term; if he continues as before, he will not be able to leave his post.'
  • 又云毎烧钱财如明旦欲送钱与某神只即先烧三十二张纸钱以求五道其神只到必获矣如寻常烧香多不达如是春秋祭祀者即不假告报也其烧时辄不得就地须以柴或 - Complex phrase describing rituals and methods for burning paper money and incense: 'he also said that every time he burned money, if he wanted to send money to a certain deity the next day, he would first burn thirty-two pieces of paper money to seek the five paths. The deity would surely arrive. If he burned incense as usual, it would not reach. For spring and autumn sacrifices, it is not necessary to report beforehand. When burning, it should not be done directly on the ground but with firewood or something else.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 如赐茶浆水粥耳茶酒不如赐浆水 - Reflects the cultural practice of offering various beverages and food items, with water being the simplest and most accessible.
  • 贫居之易办 - Reflects the cultural understanding that certain tasks are easier to manage in poverty, emphasizing practicality and resourcefulness.
  • 毎晚则备浆水及粥纸钱三五张 - Reflects the cultural practice of preparing offerings for the deceased, including water, porridge, and paper money.
  • 月十日后毎来皆语言商议出拔李氏 - Reflects the cultural practice of regular meetings to discuss and plan assistance for family members or community members.
  • 李氏初毎归来并不敢言 - Reflects the cultural understanding that individuals may be cautious or silent when returning from certain situations, possibly due to fear or respect.
  • 自使人同和兼许微说间事 - Reflects the cultural practice of controlled communication, where personal matters are discussed in a harmonious and limited manner.
  • 常言人罪之重者无如枉法杀人而取金帛 - Reflects the cultural understanding of the severity of certain crimes, particularly those involving corruption and violence.
  • 又曰布施者不必造佛寺不如先救骨肉间饥寒 - Reflects the cultural emphasis on immediate family support over broader charitable acts, emphasizing the importance of kinship.
  • 如有余即分锡类更有余则救街衢间也其福最大 - Reflects the cultural belief in the moral value of helping others, especially those in immediate need, and the idea that such actions bring the greatest merit.
  • 郑君兼凭问还往间一人寿命官爵回报云此人好受金帛今被折寿已欲尽矣然更有一官如能改即得终此秩若踵前则不离任矣 - Reflects the cultural understanding of the consequences of accepting bribes and the possibility of redemption through change.
  • 又云毎烧钱财如明旦欲送钱与某神只即先烧三十二张纸钱以求五道其神只到必获矣如寻常烧香多不达如是春秋祭祀者即不假告报也其烧时辄不得就地须以柴或 - Reflects the cultural practice of burning paper money and incense as offerings to deities, with specific rituals and methods to ensure the effectiveness of the offerings.

Proper Nouns

  • 郑君 - Proper noun referring to a person named Zheng.
  • 李氏 - Proper noun referring to a person or family named Li.
  • 五道 - Proper noun referring to the five paths in Chinese religious and philosophical contexts.

[4449]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 草荐之从一头以火爇不得剔其钱即不破碎一一可达也 - Describes a method of burning grass to extract coins without breaking them; '草荐' refers to a type of grass or straw, '爇' means to burn, and '剔其钱' means to extract the coins. The phrase '一一可达也' suggests that this method is effective and reliable.
  • 至八月中李却回忽喜曰已有计可脱矣 - Indicates that someone named Li returns in August and is suddenly happy, saying he has a plan to escape; '却回' means to return, '忽喜' means to suddenly become happy, and '已有计可脱矣' means he has a plan to escape.
  • 郑询之曰奈何然须致纸钱三五万令他行下可矣 - Shows that Zheng asks Li what to do, and Li suggests that they need to provide 30,000 to 50,000 paper money to make the plan work; '郑询之' means Zheng asks, '奈何' means what to do, and '须致纸钱三五万' means they need to provide 30,000 to 50,000 paper money.
  • 自是追呼稍稀或十日方一去但云磨勘文案未毕所言受罪亦不见其余但拷问科决而已 - Indicates that after the plan is implemented, the frequency of calls and visits decreases, and they only go every ten days, saying they are still reviewing the documents; '自是' means from then on, '追呼稍稀' means the calls and visits become less frequent, '磨勘文案未毕' means they are still reviewing the documents, and '所言受罪亦不见其余但拷问科决而已' means they only mention the punishment and do not talk about other things, but only ask questions and make decisions.
  • 又尝言当邑某坊曲某姓名人合至某月日卒至时更无差谬 - Suggests that someone once said that a person from a certain street in the town would die on a certain date, and it happened exactly as predicted; '尝言' means once said, '当邑某坊曲' means a certain street in the town, '某姓名人' means a person with a certain name, '合至某月日卒' means they would die on a certain date, and '至时更无差谬' means it happened exactly as predicted.
  • 郑君自云某即合得摄安豊尉至明年正月三日果为崔中丞邀摄安豊县尉皆其妻素知之自正月已后更免其追呼矣 - Shows that Zheng himself said he would be appointed as the magistrate of Anfeng, and it happened on January 3rd of the following year, as his wife had known all along; '郑君自云' means Zheng himself said, '某即合得摄安豊尉' means he would be appointed as the magistrate of Anfeng, '至明年正月三日果为崔中丞邀摄安豊县尉' means it happened on January 3rd of the following year, and '皆其妻素知之自正月已后更免其追呼矣' means his wife had known all along, and from January onwards, they were no longer called.
  • 郑君自有记录四十余纸此略而言也 - Indicates that Zheng has his own records of more than forty sheets, and this is a brief account; '郑君自有记录四十余纸' means Zheng has his own records of more than forty sheets, and '此略而言也' means this is a brief account.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 草荐 - Refers to a type of grass or straw, possibly used in rituals or as a material for burning.
  • 纸钱 - Refers to paper money used in funerary rituals, often burned as offerings to the deceased.
  • 磨勘文案 - Refers to the process of reviewing and verifying official documents, often associated with bureaucratic procedures.
  • 摄安豊尉 - Refers to the position of magistrate of Anfeng, a specific administrative role in ancient China.
  • 博异记 - The title of the text, which suggests it contains unusual or extraordinary stories.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The phrase '草荐之从一头以火爇不得剔其钱即不破碎一一可达也' is a bit elliptical, but it describes a method of burning grass to extract coins without breaking them. The phrase '一一可达也' suggests that this method is effective and reliable.
  • The phrase '至八月中李却回忽喜曰已有计可脱矣' is a bit elliptical, but it indicates that someone named Li returns in August and is suddenly happy, saying he has a plan to escape.
  • The phrase '郑询之曰奈何然须致纸钱三五万令他行下可矣' is a bit elliptical, but it shows that Zheng asks Li what to do, and Li suggests that they need to provide 30,000 to 50,000 paper money to make the plan work.
  • The phrase '自是追呼稍稀或十日方一去但云磨勘文案未毕所言受罪亦不见其余但拷问科决而已' is a bit elliptical, but it indicates that after the plan is implemented, the frequency of calls and visits decreases, and they only go every ten days, saying they are still reviewing the documents.
  • The phrase '又尝言当邑某坊曲某姓名人合至某月日卒至时更无差谬' is a bit elliptical, but it suggests that someone once said that a person from a certain street in the town would die on a certain date, and it happened exactly as predicted.
  • The phrase '郑君自云某即合得摄安豊尉至明年正月三日果为崔中丞邀摄安豊县尉皆其妻素知之自正月已后更免其追呼矣' is a bit elliptical, but it shows that Zheng himself said he would be appointed as the magistrate of Anfeng, and it happened on January 3rd of the following year, as his wife had known all along.
  • The phrase '郑君自有记录四十余纸此略而言也' is a bit elliptical, but it indicates that Zheng has his own records of more than forty sheets, and this is a brief account.

Proper Nouns

  • 草荐 - A type of grass or straw.
  • 纸钱 - Paper money used in funerary rituals.
  • 磨勘文案 - The process of reviewing and verifying official documents.
  • 摄安豊尉 - The position of magistrate of Anfeng.
  • 博异记 - The title of the text, which suggests it contains unusual or extraordinary stories.

[4450]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 太平广记 - A comprehensive collection of tales and anecdotes compiled during the Song Dynasty.
  • 钦定四库全书 - A monumental collection of Chinese literature and scholarship, compiled under imperial auspices.
  • 大业中 - Refers to the era of Emperor Yang of Sui, known as the 'Great Cause' period.
  • 雍州长安县人 - Indicates the geographical and administrative context of the story, referring to a person from Chang'an County in Yongzhou (modern-day Xi'an).
  • 缩一脚 - A colloquial expression meaning 'shrink one leg,' used here to describe an unusual physical state.
  • 大殓 - The final stage of funeral rites, involving the dressing of the body before burial.
  • 受持金刚般若经 - Refers to the practice of holding and reciting the *Diamond Sutra*, a key Buddhist text.
  • 善哉 - A common Buddhist expression of approval or praise.
  • 受罪之处 - A euphemism for the afterlife or hell, where one suffers for their sins.
  • 极大辛苦 - Describes extreme hardship or suffering.
  • 不可具述 - Means 'cannot be fully described,' indicating the severity of the suffering.
  • 诸畜生 - Refers to animals, often in a negative or derogatory context.
  • 债命 - Literally 'debt of life,' suggesting a karmic debt or obligation.
  • 何讳 - Means 'why are you ashamed' or 'why do you hide this,' implying a sense of guilt or shame.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 隋大业 - The reign period of Emperor Yang of Sui (581-618), known for its ambitious construction projects and military campaigns.
  • 雍州长安县 - Refers to Chang'an County in Yongzhou, which is now part of modern-day Xi'an, the capital of Shaanxi Province.
  • 金刚般若经 - The *Diamond Sutra* is a central text in Mahayana Buddhism, emphasizing the concept of emptiness and the impermanence of all things.
  • 王 - Refers to a judge or official in the afterlife, who questions the deceased about their actions in life.
  • 大地狱 - A term used in Buddhist cosmology to describe a place of severe punishment for those who have committed grave sins.
  • 镬汤苦具 - Describes the instruments of torture in hell, including cauldrons of boiling oil and other cruel devices.
  • 猪羊鸡鸭 - Common domestic animals often used as metaphors for various types of beings or entities in Chinese literature.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This sentence is complex and requires careful parsing. It describes the sequence of events: the person died seven days ago, the family was preparing for the final funeral rites, and the body suddenly shrunk one leg, causing fear and hesitation.
  • The question posed by the judge in the afterlife, asking about the person's good deeds and actions in life.
  • The judge acknowledges the person's good deeds but also shows them the place of punishment, indicating a balance between merit and sin.
  • The animals gather around the person, demanding their 'debt of life,' which suggests a karmic connection.
  • The person's defense against the animals, stating that they did not eat them and questioning why they are being pursued.

Proper Nouns

  • 赵文若 - The name of the protagonist, a person who experienced a miraculous return to life.
  • 金刚般若经 - The *Diamond Sutra*, a key Buddhist text.
  • 大业 - The reign period of Emperor Yang of Sui.
  • 雍州长安县 - The location of the story, now part of modern-day Xi'an.
  • 王 - The judge or official in the afterlife.
  • 大地狱 - A place of severe punishment in Buddhist cosmology.
  • 猪羊鸡鸭 - Common domestic animals used in the story as metaphors for various beings.

[4451]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 一心念佛深悔诸罪 - Describes intense religious devotion and penitence, focusing on chanting Buddha's name and deeply repenting sins.
  • 求为修福报谢诸畜各散 - Reflects the Buddhist concept of karma and the importance of making amends by accumulating merit and repaying the animals, who then disperse.
  • 使人将文若却至王所 - Implies a form of punishment or trial, where someone takes 文若 (Wen Ruo) back to the king's presence.
  • 然后放回文若得苏具说此事然患头痛及手足久后修福痛渐得差 - Describes the aftermath of the punishment, where 文若 (Wen Ruo) is released, regains consciousness, and recounts the event, suffering from headaches and limb pain that gradually improve after accumulating more merit.
  • 但见道俗亲疎并劝受持 - Shows 文若 (Wen Ruo)'s dedication to spreading the teachings, encouraging both monks and laypeople, regardless of their closeness or distance, to recite and uphold the Diamond Sutra.
  • 后因使至一驿防上暂时偃息于时梦见一青衣妇女急来乞命 - Describes the setting and the dream, where 文若 (Wen Ruo) travels to a post station, takes a short rest, and has a dream of a young woman in green clothing urgently asking for help.
  • 文若惊寤即唤驿长问曰汝为吾欲杀生不驿长答云实为公欲杀一小羊文若问云其羊作何色答云是青防羊 - Describes the interaction between 文若 (Wen Ruo) and the postmaster, where 文若 (Wen Ruo) wakes up in shock and calls the postmaster, asking if he was going to kill an animal. The postmaster confirms that he intended to kill a small sheep, which was described as green.
  • 文若报云汝急放却吾与价直赎取放之良由般若威力防资感应也 - Describes 文若 (Wen Ruo)'s response to the postmaster, offering to buy the sheep and set it free, attributing this action to the power of the Diamond Sutra and the merit he has accumulated.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 文若 - Refers to Wen Ruo, a historical figure known for his religious devotion and miraculous experiences.
  • 金刚般若 - Refers to the Diamond Sutra, a significant Buddhist text that emphasizes the concept of emptiness and the impermanence of all things.
  • 王 - Refers to a king, likely a ruler of a kingdom where 文若 (Wen Ruo) lived.
  • 青衣妇女 - Refers to a young woman in green clothing, which is a specific detail in the dream.
  • 青防羊 - Refers to a small green sheep, which is a specific detail in the story.
  • 般若威力 - Refers to the power of wisdom (般若) in Buddhism, which is believed to have the ability to transform circumstances and bring about positive outcomes.

Proper Nouns

  • 文若 - The name of the historical figure.
  • 金刚般若 - The title of the Diamond Sutra.
  • 王 - The title of the king.
  • 青衣妇女 - The description of the young woman in the dream.
  • 青防羊 - The description of the small green sheep.

[4452]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 暴病死 - Sudden and unexpected death.
  • 一日而蘓 - Miraculous recovery after one day.
  • 被至官所 - Taken by spirits to the place of officials, indicating a supernatural experience.
  • 问何故杀牛两头 - Asked why he killed two cows, a direct question about the cause of his death.
  • 不杀官曰汝弟证汝杀何故不承 - He said he did not kill them; the official said, 'Your brother testified that you killed them, why did you not confess?'
  • 既至枷械甚严 - After arriving, he was severely shackled, indicating harsh treatment.
  • 汝所言兄杀牛虚实 - The truth or falsehood of what your brother said about your killing the cows, questioning the validity of the testimony.
  • 使弟杀牛防是实然国事也 - It is true that I had my brother kill the cows for the purpose of appeasing the barbarians, but it was a matter of state, justifying the action as a state duty.
  • 因谓恪弟曰汝以证兄故久留汝兄既遣杀汝便无罪放任受生 - The official then said to the younger brother, 'Because you testified against your brother, you have been detained. Now that your brother has ordered your execution, you are free to be reborn,' explaining the fate of the younger brother.
  • 因何复杀两鸭 - Why did you also kill two ducks? another question about the cause of death.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 獠贼 - Refers to the ethnic minorities in the southern regions of China, often described as 'barbarians' or 'savages' in historical texts.
  • 招慰 - Refers to the act of recruiting and pacifying the barbarians, a common practice in the Tang Dynasty to maintain control over frontier regions.
  • 寒食 - Refers to the Cold Food Festival, a traditional Chinese festival observed before the Qingming Festival, where cold food is eaten to commemorate the dead.

Proper Nouns

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 執是市吏殺君第不承事當必釋 - Complex and elliptical phrase. '執是' means 'to hold or arrest this,' '市吏' refers to a market official, '殺君第' means 'killed the lord's household,' and '不承事當必釋' suggests 'not being held accountable and must be released.' Describes a situation where a market official arrested someone for killing the lord's household but was not held accountable and was released.
  • 須臾王坐主簿引齡入 - '須臾' means 'in a short while,' '王坐' refers to the king's seat or throne, '主簿' is a low-ranking official, and '引齡入' means 'led Leng into.' Describes a situation where a low-ranking official led Leng into the presence of the king shortly after.
  • 市吏便引適以诘问云实求肠不遣杀驴言讫见市吏枷项在前有驴羊鸡豕数十辈随其后 - Long, complex sentence. '市吏便引適以诘问' means 'the market official immediately led and questioned,' '云实求肠不遣杀驴' means 'said he really wanted the intestines and did not let go of the donkey,' and '言讫见市吏枷项在前有驴羊鸡豕数十辈随其后' means 'after speaking, he saw the market official with shackles on his neck, with dozens of donkeys, sheep, chickens, and pigs following behind.' Describes a situation where the market official was questioned, claimed he wanted the intestines, and was then seen with shackles on his neck, followed by animals.
  • 王言此人尚有数政官录不可久留宜速放去若更迟延恐形骸隳壊 - '王言' means 'the king said,' '此人尚有数政官录' means 'this person still has several political records,' '不可久留' means 'cannot be kept for long,' '宜速放去' means 'should be quickly released,' and '若更迟延恐形骸隳壊' means 'if delayed further, his body might deteriorate.' Suggests that the king believed the person had unresolved issues and should be released quickly before his health deteriorates.
  • 今放君回当万计修福龄再拜出王复令呼谓主簿可领此人观诸地狱主簿令引龄前行入小孔中见牛头卒以叉刺人随业受罪龄不肯观出小孔辞主簿毕复往别吏吏云我本戸部令史一人曰我本京兆府史久在地府求生人间不得君可为写金 - Long, complex sentence. '今放君回当万计修福' means 'now that you are being released, you should do your utmost to cultivate virtue,' '龄再拜出' means 'Leng bowed twice and left,' '王复令呼谓主簿可领此人观诸地狱' means 'the king then ordered the main clerk to lead this person to see the various hells,' '主簿令引龄前行入小孔中' means 'the main clerk led Leng forward into a small hole,' '见牛头卒以叉刺人随业受罪' means 'he saw ox-headed officers using spears to pierce people, suffering according to their karma,' '龄不肯观出小孔辞主簿毕复往别吏' means 'Leng refused to look and came out of the hole, bidding farewell to the main clerk, and then went to see another official,' '吏云我本戸部令史一人曰我本京兆府史久在地府求生人间不得君可为写金' means 'the official said, 'I was originally a clerk in the Ministry of Household, and one person said, 'I was originally a clerk in the Capital Prefecture, and I have been in the underworld for a long time, unable to be reborn in the human world. You can write gold for me.'' Describes a journey through hell and the request for a good deed to be done in the human world.

  • 随业受罪 - Suffering according to one's karma, a concept from Buddhist and Taoist beliefs about the consequences of one's actions.
  • 写金 - Writing gold, a metaphor for performing a good deed or making a donation to improve one's karma.

[4459]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 光明经法华维摩湼盘等经 - Refers to several important Mahayana Buddhist scriptures: *Gāngmíng jīng* (光明经), *Huáhuà jīng* (法华经), *Wéimó jīng* (维摩经), and *Nípán jīng* (湼盘经).
  • 兼为设斋度我 - Means 'to also set up a vegetarian meal to liberate me.' *设斋* refers to setting up a vegetarian meal, often for religious purposes, and *度我* means to liberate or save oneself.
  • 即得生人间龄悉许之 - Means 'I will immediately be reborn in the human realm, and all my wishes will be granted.' *即得生人间* means 'I will immediately be reborn in the human realm,' and *龄悉许之* means 'all my wishes will be granted.'
  • 吏复求金银钱各三千贯 - Means 'the official then asks for 3,000 strings of gold, silver, and copper coins.' *吏* refers to an official, *求* means to ask for, and *贯* is a unit of currency.
  • 京官贫穷实不能办 - Means 'the official in the capital is poor and truly cannot afford it.' *京官* refers to an official in the capital, *贫穷* means poor, and *实不能办* means truly cannot afford.
  • 金钱者是世间黄钱银钱者白钱耳 - Means 'gold coins are yellow  coins, and silver coins are white  coins.' ** is a phonetic transcription of a term that is unclear, possibly referring to a type of coin or currency.
  • 若求钱当亦可办不知何所送之 - Means 'if you seek  coins, it can also be done, but I don't know where to send them.' *钱* is the same term as mentioned above.
  • 世作钱于都市其钱多为地府所 - Means 'in the world, money is made in the city, but most of it is taken by the underworld.' *世作钱* means 'money is made in the world,' *都市* means city, *地府* refers to the underworld, and ** is a phonetic transcription of a term that is unclear, possibly meaning 'taken' or 'seized.'
  • 君可呼凿钱人于家中密室作之毕可以袋盛当于水际焚之 - Means 'you can call the money-maker to your home, have them make it in a secret room, and once done, put it in a bag and burn it at the water's edge.' *凿钱人* means 'money-maker,' *密室* means 'secret room,' *袋盛* means 'put in a bag,' and *水际焚之* means 'burn it at the water's edge.'
  • 受钱之时若横风动灰即是我得 - Means 'when receiving the money, if a crosswind blows the ashes, it is mine.' *受钱之时* means 'when receiving the money,' *横风动灰* means 'a crosswind blows the ashes,' and *是我得* means 'it is mine.'
  • 若有风飏灰即为地府及他神所受 - Means 'if the wind scatters the ashes, it is received by the underworld and other spirits.' *风飏灰* means 'the wind scatters the ashes,' and *地府及他神所受* means 'received by the underworld and other spirits.'
  • 此亦宜为常占 - Means 'this should also be a regular practice.' *常占* means 'regular practice.'
  • 然神常苦饥烧钱之时可兼设少佳酒饭以两束草立席上 - Means 'since ghosts and spirits often suffer from hunger, when burning money, you can also set up a little good wine and food, and place two bundles of grass on the mat.' *然神常苦饥* means 'since ghosts and spirits often suffer from hunger,' *烧钱之时* means 'when burning money,' *设少佳酒饭* means 'set up a little good wine and food,' and *两束草立席上* means 'place two bundles of grass on the mat.'
  • 我得映草而坐亦得食也 - Means 'I can sit under the grass and also eat.' *映草而坐* means 'sit under the grass,' and *亦得食也* means 'also eat.'
  • 辞讫行数里至舍见家人哭泣因尔觉痛遍身恍惚迷闷久之开视遂活 - Means 'after finishing the conversation, he walked several miles to his house, saw his family crying, and then felt pain all over his body, becoming confused and unconscious for a long time. After opening his eyes, he revived.' *辞讫* means 'after finishing the conversation,' *行数里* means 'walked several miles,' *至舍* means 'to his house,' *见家人哭泣* means 'saw his family crying,' *因尔觉痛遍身* means 'then felt pain all over his body,' *恍惚迷闷久之* means 'became confused and unconscious for a long time,' and *开视遂活* means 'after opening his eyes, he revived.'
  • 造经像及烧钱毕十数日平复如常 - Means 'after making the statues and burning the money, after several days, he recovered as usual.' *造经像* means 'making the statues,' *烧钱毕* means 'after burning the money,' *十数日* means 'several days,' and *平复如常* means 'recovered as usual.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 光明经法华维摩湼盘等经 - These are important Mahayana Buddhist scriptures.
  • 设斋度我 - Setting up a vegetarian meal for religious purposes is a common practice in Buddhism.
  • 地府 - The underworld in Chinese mythology, where spirits and the dead go.
  • 钱 - An unclear term, possibly referring to a type of coin or currency.
  • 凿钱人 - A person who makes money, possibly a mint worker.
  • 烧钱 - Burning paper money as a ritual offering to the dead or spirits is a common practice in Chinese culture.
  • 神 - Ghosts and spirits in Chinese folklore.
  • 常占 - Regular practice or ritual.
  • 佳酒饭 - Good wine and food, often offered as a ritual offering.
  • 草立席上 - Placing grass on a mat, possibly for the spirits to sit on.

  • 地府 - The underworld.

  • 见如官曹舍者数处不肯受之 - Literal translation: 'Saw several official residences that would not accept him.' Refers to official residences or offices refusing to accept the deceased.
  • 从西转来无持引者而转驶如风 - Literal translation: 'Turned swiftly like the wind, without being held or guided.' Describes rapid movement of a metaphorical or literal wheel.
  • 仰首见天有孔不觉倐尔上升以头穿中 - Literal translation: 'Looked up and saw a hole in the sky, suddenly rose, and passed through it.' Describes a sudden change in status or location.
  • 两手抟两边四向顾视 - Literal translation: 'Grasped the sides and looked around.' Describes the monk's actions while on the ship.
  • 疲而复下所将衡去人笑曰见何物不能上乎 - Literal translation: 'Tired and fell back down, the person he was carrying laughed and said, 'What did you see that you couldn't ascend?' Describes the failed attempt to ascend.
  • 乃以衡付船官船官行船使为舵工 - Literal translation: 'Then handed him over to the ship official, who became a steersman.' Describes the transfer of responsibility.
  • 不晓捉舵跄沙洲上吏司推衡以法 - Literal translation: 'Did not know how to hold the rudder, stumbled onto a sandbar, and was pushed according to the law.' Describes the consequences of the monk's actions.

  • 出入如人唯沉静异于畴日 - Means 'she moved in and out like an ordinary person, but was only calm and reserved, different from before.'
  • 使于桂府七旬乃还 - Means 'she was sent to the Gueifuguan (a government office) for seventy days and then returned.'

  • 计其再生才百日矣 - Means 'it was calculated that she had been reborn for about a hundred days.'
  • 或曰有物凭焉 - Means 'some said that there was something possessing her.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 鄭氏肅宗時為潤州刺史 - During the reign of Emperor Suzong, the Zheng family held the position of governor of Runzhou.
  • 七夕夜陳香筵祈于織女 - On the night of the Qixi Festival, she set up a fragrant banquet to pray to the Weaving Maid (Venus). The Qixi Festival is a traditional Chinese festival celebrating the meeting of the Cowherd and the Weaving Maid, two star-crossed lovers.
  • 吾織女汝求何福 - I am the Weaving Maid. What blessing do you seek? The Weaving Maid, also known as the Goddess of Spinning, is a prominent figure in Chinese mythology.
  • 願工巧耳 - I only wish for skillfulness. This reflects the cultural value placed on craftsmanship and skill in traditional Chinese society.
  • 乃遺一金針長寸余 - She then gave a golden needle, about an inch long. The golden needle is a symbol of divine favor and skill.
  • 置裙帶中令三日勿語汝當竒巧不爾化成男子 - She placed it in her skirt belt and instructed her to remain silent for three days. If you do, you will become exceptionally skilled; otherwise, you will turn into a man. This is a common motif in Chinese folklore, where breaking a condition leads to a transformation.
  • 張數女皆卒 - Several of Zhang's daughters died. This recurring theme of death suggests a curse or supernatural influence.
  • 忽病而不言 - She suddenly fell ill and became silent. This indicates a supernatural or mystical event.
  • 聞藥至情急是以呼耳 - Hearing that medicine was coming, she became desperate and called out. This shows the urgency and desperation of the situation.
  • 母異之乃不服藥 - Her mother found this strange and decided not to give her the medicine. This reflects the mother's intuition and the supernatural elements of the story.
  • 尋卒既葬母悲念乃常所戲之物而匿之 - Soon after, she died. After her burial, her mother, grieving, hid the things she used to play with. This shows the mother's grief and the importance of the objects to her.
  • 未逾月遂生一男人 - Within a month, she gave birth to a boy. This introduces a new character and suggests a supernatural event.
  • 兒啼哭張氏哭女其兒亦哭罷即止 - The boy cried, and Zhang cried for her daughter, and the boy stopped crying. This shows the emotional connection between the characters.
  • 及能言常戲弄之物 - When he could speak, he often played with the things she used to play with. This shows the boy's attachment to his sister.
  • 官至柱史 - He later became a high-ranking official. This describes the boy's future career and success.
  • 出史遺 - This story is recorded in *Shi Yi* (Records of the Unusual). *Shi Yi* is a collection of strange and unusual stories from Chinese history and folklore.

Only one man is present, the rest are women, some sitting, some lying down, and some standing. Their clothes and appearances are indistinguishable from living people. The coffin and burial objects are decayed. A white fox child is seen, and when chased, it bites the left foot of the king.   • 舒绰东阳人稽古博文尤以隂阳留意 - Introduces Shu Chuo, a man from Dongyang who was well-versed in ancient knowledge and had a particular interest in divination and feng shui.

  • 吏部侍郎杨恭仁欲改葬观王求善图墓者五六人 - Describes an official named Yang Gongren who wanted to find a suitable location for the reburial of a king and sought out several experts in tombography.
  • 并称海内名手停于宅共论蓺互相是非 - Indicates that the experts were all considered top-tier in their field and debated among themselves at Yang's residence.
  • 恭仁莫知孰是乃遣防解者驰往京师于欲之原取所拟之地四处各作歴记其方面髙下形势各取一斗土并歴封之 - Describes Yang Gongren sending someone to the capital to gather soil samples from four different locations proposed for the new burial site.

  • 此所拟处深五尺之外有五谷若得一谷即是福地公侯世世不絶 - Indicates that Shu Chuo believed that the selected location had favorable conditions for the reburial, predicting good fortune for future generations.
  • 恭仁即将绰向京令人掘深七尺得一穴如五石瓮大有粟七八防 - Describes Yang Gongren having Shu Chuo excavate the site, where they found a large cavity filled with grain.

  • The sentence '歴览三日而无获夜宿村舍时冬寒室内惟一榻式则籍地士龙据榻以憩' describes their three-day exploration and their arrangement in a village house, with Zhang Shi sleeping on the floor and Zhou Shilong sitting on the bed.

  • 之土无情也遇雨而圮偶然也 - The land is无情 (without emotion), and when it meets rain, it collapses by chance. The term '无情' (without emotion) is somewhat ambiguous in this context.
  • 穷象数者已悬定于十八万六千四百日之前 - Those who study the principles of numbers and images have already determined (the fate) in advance for eighteen thousand six hundred forty days before.
  • 矧于理乱之运穷达之命 - How much more so the changes of order and chaos, the fate of prosperity and adversity.
  • 圣贤不逢君臣偶合 - Saints and sages do not meet, and the coincidence of ruler and minister.
  • 姜牙得璜而尚父仲尼无凤而旅人傅説梦达于岩埜子房神授于圯上 - Lists several historical figures and their circumstances, such as Jiang Ziya receiving a jade disc and being elevated to the position of a father, Confucius not meeting a phoenix and traveling as a commoner, Fu Yue dreaming of reaching the wilds, and Zhang Liang receiving divine instruction at the bridge.
  • 岂大圣大贤犹惑于性命之理欤 - How could great saints and sages still be confused by the principles of life and destiny?
  • 将凂身存教示人道之不可废欤 - Or is it to preserve teachings and show that human ways cannot be abandoned?
  • 余不可得而知之 - I cannot know it.
  • 钦悦寻自右阙歴殿中侍御史为时宰李林甫所恶斥摈于外不显其身 - I, Qinyue, subsequently served as the Right Gatekeeper and then as a Censor in the Palace, but was disliked by the prime minister Li Linfu and expelled from court, not being able to show my face.
  • 故余叙其所闻系于二篇之后以着筮之神明聪哲之悬解竒偶之有数贻诸好事为后学之竒翫焉 - Therefore, I have recorded what I have heard and appended it to the end of these two chapters to demonstrate the divination's divine wisdom, the suspension of understanding, and the odd and even numbers, to be enjoyed by those who love learning and to be a wonder for later scholars.
  • 时贞元九年十一月二十八日赵郡李吉甫记 - On the twenty-eighth day of the eleventh month in the ninth year of the Zhenyuan era, Li Jifu of Zhao Prefecture recorded this.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 姜牙 - Jiang Ziya, a legendary figure who served as a minister to King Wen and King Wu of Zhou.
  • 尚父 - A title given to Jiang Ziya, meaning 'elder father.'
  • 仲尼 - Confucius, the great Chinese philosopher.
  • 旅人 - A traveler or wanderer.
  • 傅説 - Fu Yue, a famous minister during the Shang Dynasty.
  • 子房 - Zhang Liang, a famous strategist during the Han Dynasty.
  • 李林甫 - Li Linfu, a notorious prime minister during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 赵郡李吉甫 - Li Jifu, a historian and politician during the Tang Dynasty.

Proper Nouns

  • 尚父 - Elder Father
  • 仲尼 - Confucius
  • 旅人 - Traveler
  • 傅説 - Fu Yue
  • 子房 - Zhang Liang
  • 李林甫 - Li Linfu
  • 赵郡李吉甫 - Li Jifu

[4599]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 泉州之南有山焉 - Standard locative phrase in Classical Chinese, meaning 'There is a mountain to the south of Quanzhou.'
  • 其山峻起壁立 - Vivid description of the mountain's steepness, meaning 'The mountain rises abruptly and stands like a wall.'
  • 下有潭水深不可测 - Describes the depth of the pool, meaning 'Below it is a deep pool of water that cannot be measured.'
  • 周十余畆 - Gives an idea of the size of the pool, meaning 'Surrounding it is over ten mu (a unit of land).'
  • 中有蛟鳄 - Introduces the dangerous creatures in the pool, meaning 'There are dragons and crocodiles in it.'
  • 尝为人患 - Indicates the threat posed by the creatures, meaning 'It often causes trouble for people.'
  • 人有误近或牛马就而饮者 - Describes the conditions under which the creatures attack, meaning 'If someone mistakenly approaches or if cattle and horses drink from it.'
  • 辄为吞食 - Describes the immediate danger, meaning 'They immediately swallow and eat them.'
  • 泉人苦之有年矣 - Indicates the long-standing problem, meaning 'The people of Quanzhou have suffered from it for many years.'
  • 由是近山居者或挈引妻子徙去他郡以逃其患 - Describes the people's response to the danger, meaning 'As a result, those who live near the mountain either take their wives and children and move to other counties to escape the danger.'
  • 元和五年一夕闻山南有雷震暴兴 - Sets the scene for the event, meaning 'In the fifth year of the Yuanhe era, one night, a violent thunderstorm erupted to the south of the mountain.'
  • 震数百里若山崩之状 - Describes the intensity of the storm, meaning 'It shook for hundreds of miles, as if the mountain was collapsing.'
  • 一郡惊惧 - Indicates the widespread fear, meaning 'The entire county was terrified.'
  • 里人洎牛马鸡犬俱失声仆地流汗体 - Describes the physical reaction to the storm, meaning 'The villagers, along with cattle, horses, chickens, and dogs, all lost their voices and fell to the ground, sweating profusely.'
  • 屋瓦交击木树颠拔 - Describes the damage caused by the storm, meaning 'The tiles of the houses clashed, and the trees were uprooted.'
  • 自戌及子雷震方息 - Gives the duration of the storm, meaning 'The thunderstorm lasted from戌 to子 (a specific time period).'
  • 明旦往视之其山摧堕石壁数百仞殆尽 - Describes the aftermath of the storm, meaning 'The next morning, they went to see, and the mountain had collapsed, with hundreds of仞 of the stone walls almost completely gone.'
  • 俱填其潭水溢流注满四野 - Describes the flooding, meaning 'They all filled the pool, and the water overflowed, spreading throughout the fields.'
  • 蛟螭之血徧若黄 - Describes the aftermath of the creatures' deaths, meaning 'The blood of the dragons and crocodiles spread throughout the fields, turning them red and yellow.'
  • 石壁之上有凿成文字一十九言 - Introduces the mysterious inscription, meaning 'On the stone walls, there were nineteen characters carved.'
  • 字势甚古 - Indicates the age of the inscription, meaning 'The characters were very ancient.'
  • 郡中士庶无能知者 - Indicates the mystery of the inscription, meaning 'The scholars and common people in the county could not understand it.'
  • 自是居人无复患矣 - Indicates the resolution of the problem, meaning 'From then on, the residents no longer suffered.'
  • 惧者既息迁者亦归结屋架庐接比其地 - Describes the return to normalcy, meaning 'Those who were afraid stopped being afraid, and those who had moved returned, building houses and connecting them to the land.'
  • 郡守因之名其地为石铭里 - Describes the naming of the place, meaning 'The prefecture governor named the place Stone Inscription Village.'
  • 盖因字为铭且识其异也 - Explains the naming, meaning 'It was named because of the characters, which marked the unusual event.'
  • 后有客于泉者能其字持至东洛 - Introduces the scholar who deciphered the inscription, meaning 'Later, a guest from Quanzhou could read the characters and brought them to Donglu (east of Luoyang).'
  • 时故吏部侍郎韩愈自尚书郎为河南令见而识之 - Introduces Han Yu, meaning 'At that time, the former Secretary of the Ministry of Personnel, Han Yu, who was serving as the magistrate of Henan, saw and recognized them.'
  • 其文曰诏赤黑示之鳄鱼天公卑杀牛人壬癸神书急急 - Content of the inscription, meaning 'The text read: 'Imperial decree, red and black, showing the crocodile. The sky is low, killing the ox-herder. The divine book is urgent, urgent.'
  • 然则详究其义似上帝责蛟螭之辞令戮其害也 - Interprets the inscription, meaning 'Thus, when carefully examined, it seems to be the words of the Supreme Deity, ordering the punishment of the dragons and crocodiles.'
  • 其字则蝌蚪篆书故泉人无有识者矣 - Explains why the inscription was mysterious, meaning 'The characters are in the style of ancient script, so the people of Quanzhou could not recognize them.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 泉州 - Quanzhou, a city in Fujian Province, China.
  • 元和五年 - The fifth year of the Yuanhe era (810 AD) during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 韩愈 - Han Yu, a prominent Tang Dynasty scholar, poet, and politician.
  • 蝌蚪篆书 - Ancient script, often referring to the earliest form of Chinese writing.
  • 宣室志 - A collection of anecdotes and stories from the Tang Dynasty.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 拒命 - Defy orders or commands; describes the rebellion of Wu Yuanji against the emperor's authority.
  • 雕虫 - Metaphor for intricate or delicate work, often used to describe calligraphy or inscriptions.
  • 己酉日 - Specific date in the Chinese calendar; in this context, refers to a day when the rebellion was successfully suppressed.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 元和元年 - The first year of the Yuanhe era during the reign of Emperor Xianzong of Tang (806 AD).
  • 淮西 - A region in present-day Henan Province, which was a frequent site of rebellion during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 吴少诚 - A military governor (jiedushi) of Huai West who died in 813, leading to a rebellion by his son Wu Yuanji.
  • 裴度 - A prominent statesman and military leader of the Tang Dynasty, known for his role in suppressing rebellions.
  • 宣室志 - A collection of stories and anecdotes from the Tang Dynasty, often containing supernatural elements.

[4601]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 黑山之隂 - Literally 'the north side of Heishan Mountain,' referring to a specific geographical location. In classical Chinese, '隂' (yīn) refers to the north side of a mountain or south side of a river.
  • 都防单于 - A title or position. '都防' may be a military or administrative title, and '单于' (chányú) is the title of the leader of the Xiongnu, a nomadic people. This may indicate that Zhang Weiqing held a high-ranking position in a frontier military or administrative system.
  • 从事卢立 - '从事' (cóngshì) is a classical term for an official or assistant, often a subordinate in a military or administrative office. '卢立' is a personal name.
  • 梦一人颀长黑衣 - '颀长' (qí cháng) means tall and slender, and '黑衣' (hēiyī) means black clothing. This is a descriptive phrase indicating a tall, dark-robed figure.
  • 子幸迁我于军城中 - A polite request: 'You would be kind to move me to the military city.'
  • 及晓立不谕 - '及晓' means by morning or upon waking. '不谕' means not understood or unclear. This indicates that the dream’s meaning was unclear to Lu Li upon waking.
  • 已而遂去 - After that, he went away. This is a narrative transition.
  • 愿新其土木之制 - '愿新其' is a polite request to renovate or rebuild. '土木之制' refers to the structure or construction of the temple.
  • 惟清喜而可其请 - '可其请' means to approve or accept the request.
  • 先是单于府以惟清有美化状其政绩 - '先是' means 'previously' or 'before that.' '美化状' is a term for a commendation or memorial (上表) of good conduct. '其政绩' refers to his administrative achievements.
  • 诏既至而未有碑石 - The imperial edict had arrived, but there was no stone for the stele yet.
  • 命使采石于云中郡未还 - '命使采石' means to send an envoy to gather stone. '云中郡' is a historical county, likely in present-day Inner Mongolia.
  • 铲其西得一石方而长 - '铲其西' means to dig on the west side. '得一石方而长' means they found a stone that was square and long.
  • 其下有刻出张字歴然可辨 - '其下有刻' means there was an inscription. '出张字' means the character '张' (Zhang) was present. '歴然可辨' means clearly visible.
  • 工人持以献惟清 - The workers brought the stone to Zhang Weiqing.
  • 天赐吾之碑石 - '天赐' means a divine gift or a gift from heaven.
  • 召从事视之 - Summoned the assistant to examine it.
  • 立且惊且异 - Lu Li was both surprised and amazed.
  • 因起贺而白前梦 - Then he rose to congratulate Zhang Weiqing and reported the dream.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 李卫公庙 - A temple dedicated to Li Wei Gong (李卫公), likely Li Jing (李靖), a famous general of the Tang dynasty. He was known as 'Weigong' (卫公) for his service to Emperor Taizong.
  • 张惟清 - A historical figure, possibly an official or military commander in the Tang dynasty.
  • 单于 - A title used for the leader of the Xiongnu, but in this context, it may refer to a frontier military commander or governor in the northern frontier.
  • 从事 - A classical term for an assistant or subordinate official, often found in military or administrative contexts.
  • 卢立 - A personal name of an assistant or subordinate to Zhang Weiqing.
  • 宣室志 - A classical Chinese collection of anecdotes and supernatural stories, attributed to the Tang dynasty author Zhang Weiqing. This is the source of the text and indicates that the story is a legend or supernatural tale.

Proper Nouns

  • 张惟清 - Zhang Weiqing, the main figure in the story.
  • 李卫公 - Li Wei Gong (李卫公), likely Li Jing, a famous general of the Tang dynasty.
  • 卢立 - Lu Li, a subordinate of Zhang Weiqing.
  • 骆忠 - Luo Zhong, a military officer sent to report to the emperor.
  • 云中郡 - Yunzhong County, a historical administrative region in northern China, likely in present-day Inner Mongolia.
  • 宣室志 - *Xuanshi Zhi* (宣室志), a classical Chinese anthology of anecdotes and supernatural stories attributed to Zhang Weiqing.

[4602]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 太和中 - Reign period of Emperor Xiaowen of Northern Wei (471–499 CE), specifically during the reign era named 'Taihe.'
  • 王璠亷問丹陽 - 'Wang Fan, the governor of DanYang, inquired about something.' The term '亷問' (inquire) is used here to indicate an official investigation or inquiry.
  • 因沟其城 - 'Because he was digging a moat around the city,' where '因' indicates a causal relationship.
  • 得一石铭文 - 'Found an inscribed stone,' where '铭文' (inscription) refers to carved writing on a stone.
  • 是不祥也 - 'This is an omen of bad fortune,' where '不祥' (omen of bad fortune) is a common idiom in Classical Chinese.
  • 夫山有石石有玉玉有瑕即休也 - 'The mountain has stones, stones have jade, jade has flaws, and then it stops,' where '夫' (fu) is a particle used to introduce a statement, and '即休也' (ji xiu ye) means 'and then it stops' or 'and then it ends.'
  • 公之先曰崟崟生础 - 'Your ancestors were called Yin Yin, who gave birth to a stone base,' where '先' (xian) means 'ancestors' and '生' (sheng) means 'gave birth to.'
  • 是山有石也 - 'This is the mountain having stones,' where '也' (ye) is a particle used to emphasize the statement.
  • 础生璠是石有玉也 - 'The stone base gave birth to Fan, who is the stone with jade,' where '生' (sheng) means 'gave birth to' and '是' (shi) means 'is.'
  • 璠之子曰瑕休是玉有瑕即休休者绝之兆推是而辨其绝绪乎 - 'Fan's son is called Xia Xiu, who is the jade with flaws and then it stops. 'Xia Xiu' is a sign of termination. From this, can we infer the termination?' where '推是而辨其绝绪乎' (tui shi er bian qi jue xu hu) means 'from this, can we infer the termination?'
  • 吏谢之叟言竟而去 - 'The official thanked the old man, who finished speaking and left,' where '谢' (xie) means 'thanked' and '言竟而去' (yan jing er qu) means 'finished speaking and left.'
  • 至太和九年冬璠卒夷其宗果符叟之觧也 - 'In the ninth year of Taihe in winter, Wang Fan died, and his clan was exterminated, which indeed matched the old man's interpretation,' where '卒' (zu) means 'died,' '夷其宗' (yi qi zong) means 'exterminated his clan,' and '果符叟之觧也' (guo fu sou zhi jie ye) means 'indeed matched the old man's interpretation.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 太和 - Reign era of Emperor Xiaowen of Northern Wei (471–499 CE), known for significant reforms and cultural developments.
  • 王璠 - A historical figure, likely a high-ranking official during the Northern Wei dynasty.
  • 丹阳 - An ancient city in China, often associated with cultural and political significance.
  • 铭文 - Inscriptions on stones, often containing historical or religious texts, which were common in ancient China.
  • 不祥 - A common term in Classical Chinese for omens of bad fortune, often used in divination and fortune-telling contexts.
  • 山有石石有玉玉有瑕即休也 - A symbolic statement that can be interpreted as a metaphor for the imperfection and eventual decline of a lineage or dynasty.
  • 公之先曰崟崟生础 - Refers to the ancestors of Wang Fan, using symbolic names to represent their lineage.
  • 是山有石也 - A metaphorical statement indicating the presence of stones in the mountain, symbolizing the foundation of a lineage.
  • 础生璠是石有玉也 - Another metaphorical statement indicating the presence of jade in the stone, symbolizing the nobility and value of the lineage.
  • 璠之子曰瑕休是玉有瑕即休休者绝之兆推是而辨其绝绪乎 - A symbolic statement indicating the flaws in the jade, which can be interpreted as a sign of the decline or end of the lineage.
  • 吏谢之叟言竟而去 - A common scene in Classical Chinese literature where an old man provides wisdom and then leaves, often symbolizing the passing of knowledge from one generation to another.
  • 至太和九年冬璠卒夷其宗果符叟之觧也 - A historical event where Wang Fan's death and the extermination of his clan are seen as fulfilling the old man's prediction, highlighting the accuracy of the old man's interpretation.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence structure is elliptical, where '亷問' (inquired) is the main verb, and '因沟其城' (because he was digging a moat around the city) is the causal clause.
  • This sentence is a series of parallel clauses, each describing a step in a symbolic sequence.
  • The sentence structure is elliptical, where '曰' (said) introduces the name of the ancestor, and '生' (gave birth to) introduces the next generation.
  • This sentence is a complex series of clauses, each building on the previous one to form a symbolic interpretation.
  • The sentence structure is elliptical, where '谢' (thanked) and '言竟而去' (finished speaking and left) are the main actions.
  • The sentence structure is elliptical, where '卒' (died) and '夷其宗' (exterminated his clan) are the main actions, and '果符叟之觧也' (indeed matched the old man's interpretation) is the concluding clause.

Proper Nouns

  • 王璠 - A historical figure, likely a high-ranking official during the Northern Wei dynasty.
  • 太和 - The reign era of Emperor Xiaowen of Northern Wei (471–499 CE).
  • 丹阳 - An ancient city in China.
  • 叟 - An old man, often used in Classical Chinese to refer to wise or knowledgeable individuals.
  • 宣室志 - The title of the work from which this passage is taken, a collection of stories and anecdotes from the Tang Dynasty.

[4603]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 南逰 - Traveling south or southern journey, referring to Liu Guang's travels.
  • 因行山道 - While traveling through the mountain path, indicating the context of his journey.
  • 防日晩 - As the day was getting late, describing the timing of the event.
  • 誤入山崦中 - Mistakenly entered a mountain ravine, indicating a deviation from the intended path.
  • 松引盤曲 - With pines winding around, describing the scenery.
  • 行数里至一石室 - After walking several miles, he came to a stone chamber, indicating the distance and the discovery.
  • 水环拥清泉交喷 - Surrounded by clear springs that intersect and spray, describing the environment.
  • 室有茵榻若人居者 - The room had mats and beds as if inhabited by people, suggesting a lived-in space.
  • 前对霞翠 - Facing a view of colorful clouds and greenery, describing the scenic beauty.
  • 举巵以饮若甘醴 - He lifted a cup to drink, and it tasted like sweet liquor, indicating the quality of the water.
  • 尽十余巵而已醉甚 - He drank more than ten cups and became very drunk, describing the effect of the water.
  • 遂偃于榻 - He then lay down on the bed, indicating his state after drinking.
  • 及晓方寤 - Until dawn, he woke up, describing the duration of his sleep.
  • 因视石壁有雕刻文字极多 - He then looked at the stone wall and saw many carved characters, indicating his discovery.
  • 遂写其字置于袖 - He then wrote down the characters and put them in his sleeve, describing his action.
  • 词曰 - The words say, introducing the inscription.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 太和 - The Tahe period (227-233 AD) during the Three Kingdoms era, specifically under Cao Wei.
  • 柳光 - The name of the main character, Liu Guang, a traveler.
  • 山崦 - A mountain ravine or valley, often associated with remote and secluded places.
  • 石室 - A stone chamber or cave, often used in classical Chinese literature to describe hidden or mysterious places.
  • 霞翠 - Colorful clouds and greenery, a common poetic description of beautiful natural scenery.
  • 巵 - A cup or goblet, often used in classical literature to describe drinking.
  • 甘醴 - Sweet liquor, often used to describe high-quality or delicious wine.

Proper Nouns

  • 柳光 - The name of the main character, Liu Guang.
  • 武之在夘 - Refers to the year '卯' (Rabbit) in the Chinese zodiac.
  • 尧王 - The legendary Emperor Yao, a sage ruler in Chinese mythology.
  • 八季 - Eight seasons, a reference to the traditional Chinese calendar.

[4604]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 来至吾里饮吾泉以醉登吾榻而寐 - Descriptive sequence meaning 'came to my village, drank my spring water, got drunk, climbed onto my bed, and fell asleep.'
  • 刻乎其壁奥乎其义人谁以辨其东平子光先阅阅而异之遂行出径数十步回望其室尽亡见矣 - Complex sentence meaning 'carved on its wall, profound in its meaning, who can discern its intent? The Eastern Pingzi Guang, after reading it repeatedly, found it strange and left. After walking out dozens of steps, he turned back to look at the room, but it was nowhere to be seen.'
  • 光究之不得有吕生者视而解之未几告曰吾尽详矣此乃得道者语也 - Narrative sequence meaning 'Guang could not figure it out. A man named Lü saw it and understood it. Soon, he said, 'I understand it all. This is the language of one who has attained the Dao.''

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 唐氏 - Refers to the Tang dynasty, one of the most significant dynasties in Chinese history.
  • 尧王 - Refers to Emperor Gaozu of Tang, the 'Divine Yao.'
  • 八季 - Refers to the second year of the reign title 'Wude.'
  • 东平子 - Refers to the narrator, who is from the Eastern Pingzi (a place name).
  • 丁未 - A year in the Chinese zodiac, the year of the Fire Rabbit.
  • 太和 - The reign title of Emperor Wenzong of Tang, known for his efforts to restore the Tang dynasty's glory.
  • 东方有兎小首元尾者 - A metaphorical reference to the name and lineage of the Lord, using the rabbit as a symbol.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sequence '来至吾里饮吾泉以醉登吾榻而寐' must be read as a continuous narrative describing a series of actions.
  • The complex sentence '刻乎其壁奥乎其义人谁以辨其东平子光先阅阅而异之遂行出径数十步回望其室尽亡见矣' must be read as a narrative sequence, with the narrator's actions and reactions clearly separated.
  • The sequence '光究之不得有吕生者视而解之未几告曰吾尽详矣此乃得道者语也' describes the narrator's inability to understand the inscription and the subsequent explanation by Lü.
  • The historical reference '夫唐氏之初建号武德武之二年其岁己卯则武之在卯其义见矣盖武德二年也' must be read as a logical sequence, with the year and the reign title clearly linked.
  • The complex sentence '尧王者谓高祖之号神尧曰八季者亦二年也我弃其寝我去其扆者言其去盖绝去之时乃武德二年也深深然高高然人不吾知又不吾谓者言其隐而人不知也由今之后二百余祀者言君之来也' must be read as a logical sequence, with the narrator's departure and the arrival of the Lord clearly linked.
  • The complex sentence '且唐氏之初今果二百余矣焰焰其光和和其始者焰焰其光谓岁在丁未也焰者火岂非南方之丙丁之谓乎未亦火之位也和和其始谓今天子建号曰太和其始盖元年也东方有兎小首元尾者叙君之名氏东方甲乙木也兔者卯也卯以附木是栁字也小首元尾者是光也经吾道来吾里言君之来也饮吾泉以醉登吾榻而寐言君之止也刻乎其壁奥乎其义谁人以辨其东平子谓其义奥而隐独吾能辨之东平吾之邑也益又信矣如是而辨果得道者之遗记也' must be read as a logical sequence, with the narrator's observations and the inscription's meaning clearly linked.

  • 西晋八年六月 - Indicates the sixth month of the eighth year of the Western Jin dynasty.
  • 飘风吹贾谧朝衣飞数百丈 - Strong winds blew and caused Jia Yi's court robes to fly hundreds of feet.
  • 明年谧诛其年十一月京都大风发屋折木 - The following year, Jia Yi was executed. In November, strong winds in the capital caused roofs to be lifted and trees to break.
  • 西晋永康元年 - Indicates the first year of the Yongkang era of the Western Jin dynasty.
  • 大风飞石沙折木 - Strong winds blew stones and sand, breaking trees.
  • 是月赵王伦矫制废贾后害张华裴頠等 - That month, Prince Zhao, Liu Lun, falsely claimed imperial authority to depose Empress Jia and harm Zhang Hua and Pei Wei, among others.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 秦始皇 - The first emperor of China, known for unifying the country and standardizing writing, weights, measures, and currency.
  • 王莽 - A usurper who briefly established the Xin dynasty, overthrew the Han dynasty, and was later overthrown himself.
  • 贾谧 - A powerful official during the Western Jin dynasty, known for his influence and eventual downfall.
  • 张华 - A prominent scholar and statesman during the Western Jin dynasty, known for his contributions to literature and governance.
  • 赵王伦 - A prince and military leader who overthrew Emperor Hui and briefly ruled as emperor, leading to widespread chaos.
  • 贾后 - Empress Jia Nanfeng, the wife of Emperor Hui, who was deposed and killed due to her political machinations.
  • 裴頠 - A scholar and statesman during the Western Jin dynasty, known for his philosophical works and political influence.
  • 许昌 - A city in Henan Province, historically significant as a capital during the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 金墉 - A city in Henan Province, historically significant as a military stronghold.
  • 长安 - The capital of the Qin, Han, and Tang dynasties, located in modern-day Shaanxi Province.
  • 南阳 - A city in Henan Province, historically significant as a base for military and political activities.
  • 昆阳 - A city in Henan Province, historically significant as the site of a major battle during the fall of the Xin dynasty.
  • 尧女舜妻 - Refers to the legendary figures of Yao's daughter and Shun's wife, who are said to have been buried at the site described.
  • 衡山 - One of the Five Sacred Mountains of China, located in Hunan Province.
  • 湘山 - A mountain in Hunan Province, historically significant for its association with the Xiang River.
  • 路堂 - A hall or building associated with roads or travel, likely a public or administrative structure.
  • 朝衣 - Court robes, worn by officials during official functions.
  • 缯轴 - Bolts of silk, often used in court ceremonies and as valuable goods.
  • 矫制 - To falsely claim imperial authority, often used to justify political actions.
  • 废贾后 - To depose Empress Jia, a significant political event during the Western Jin dynasty.
  • 害张华裴頠等 - To harm or kill Zhang Hua and Pei Wei, prominent figures during the Western Jin dynasty.

Proper Nouns

  • 秦始皇 - Qin Shi Huang, the first emperor of China.
  • 王莽 - Wang Mang, a usurper who briefly established the Xin dynasty.
  • 贾谧 - Jia Yi, a powerful official during the Western Jin dynasty.
  • 张华 - Zhang Hua, a prominent scholar and statesman during the Western Jin dynasty.
  • 赵王伦 - Prince Zhao, Liu Lun, a prince and military leader who overthrew Emperor Hui.
  • 贾后 - Empress Jia Nanfeng, the wife of Emperor Hui.
  • 裴頠 - Pei Wei, a scholar and statesman during the Western Jin dynasty.
